message:
test(fuzz): seed the fuzz_cli_argv corpus with 222 discovered inputs (~880KB)

The fuzz_cli_argv target exists but was running with an empty
seed corpus, so every local or CI fuzz session had to rediscover
the trivial argv-parser edge cases from scratch before it could
reach anything genuinely interesting. This is the standard pattern
for cass-repo fuzz corpora (fuzz_config/, fuzz_decrypt/, fuzz_kdf/,
fuzz_manifest/ are all checked in), and fuzz_cli_argv just hadn't
been populated yet.

This commit adds the 222-file corpus that the cargo-fuzz runner
accumulated across one initial discovery pass, at the usual
SHA1-named layout used by libFuzzer. Total on-disk footprint is
about 880KB, in line with the existing committed corpora:
fuzz_config (~5MB), fuzz_manifest (~105KB), fuzz_query_parser
(~83KB), fuzz_decrypt / fuzz_kdf (~1.5KB each).

Rationale for committing the full discovered corpus rather than a
trimmed subset:

- libFuzzer's coverage-guided reduction already keeps only inputs
  that hit novel edges, so each SHA1 file here represents a
  distinct code path through argv parsing. Pruning by hand would
  risk silently losing edge coverage we've already paid for.
- 880KB is cheap in repo terms and makes every future fuzz run
  (including CI smoke runs with short time budgets) start from a
  rich basis instead of an empty one. On a 60s CI slice this
  can mean the difference between "found nothing new" and "hit
  a real regression in the first second".
- The filenames are content-addressed (SHA1 of the input bytes),
  so re-runs of the same corpus are idempotent — commits don't
  churn even if the runner re-emits identical seeds.

No fuzz_target or production code touched.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
